page: Amazon Web Services m5n.2xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The configuration includes 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across several standard operations. The instance achieves average ratings in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU and memory scores, and Redis SET operations. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks also fall within the average range. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by bottom-25% results in stress-ng single-core, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core scores also rank in the weak tier.

This instance is suited for workloads requiring balanced compute and memory resources with enhanced networking capabilities, such as static web serving and database operations, which both scored in the average tier. It is less suited for compute-heavy tasks like ray tracing, image processing, or large-scale LLM inference, where the Intel Xeon 8259CL processor exhibits poor performance, particularly with 7B parameter models. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached EBS volumes, utilizing the instance's 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services m5n.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance optimized for network and EBS workloads. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores) clocked at 3.1 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show average capabilities for multi-core CPU tasks, memory bandwidth, and Redis SET operations, though single-core execution and compression tasks fall into the weak category. With an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, and database operations that do not require high-performance local storage or intensive single-threaded processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, optimized for network workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 general-purpose instance with balanced memory and enhanced network bandwidth for standard database and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 3.1 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 4.0 GB per core
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor optimization
Average performance in database operations, Redis SET, and static web serving
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Weak single-core and compression performance relative to the hardware class
